channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police Officer Abducted Near Dama Dola Found Dead in Bajaur
item.description: In Bajaur district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, a tragic incident unfolded when Shah Munir Khan, a 54-year-old policeman, was found murdered a day after being abducted. According to officials, Munir Khan, w...

item.title: Hyderabad’s Historical and Cultural Links with Persia
item.description: The city of Hyderabad has deep-rooted connections with Persian culture that date back several centuries. From the architecture of iconic structures like Charminar, designed by Iranian immigrant Mir Mo...

item.title: UK Embassy Advises Citizens Against Filming Missile Strikes in UAE
item.description: The British Embassy in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) has issued a warning to British citizens in the region, urging them not to take photographs or share images relating to the impact of Iranian miss...

item.title: SANDF Deployed to Support SAPS in Combating Illegal Mining and Gang Violence
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has announced the deployment of 2 200 members of the South African National Defence Force (SANDF) to work alongside the South African Police Service (SAPS) in five provinces:...

item.title: Trailer Released for ‘Muthu Engira Kaattaan’, a Tamil Mystery Series Starring Vijay Sethupathi
item.description: The much-awaited trailer for the Tamil series ‘Muthu Engira Kaattaan’ has finally been released, creating a buzz among fans of mystery dramas. Helmed by acclaimed director M Manikandan and produced in...

item.title: Tanzania’s public hospitals vulnerable as AI rollout outpaces cybersecurity measures
item.description: Tanzania is pushing to introduce artificial intelligence (AI) in public hospitals, but many facilities dey without proper cyber safeguards, experts warn. IT auditors show say just one ransomware attac...

item.title: Daniel Day-Lewis Returns in 'Anemone', a Family Drama About Estranged Brothers
item.description: Anemone is a contemplative family drama exploring the complex and often unspoken dynamics between two brothers, Jem and Ray, after a twenty-year estrangement. Ray, played by Daniel Day-Lewis, lives a ...

item.title: Rastafarians Seek Legal Recognition for Religious Use of Bhang
item.description: Members of Kenya’s Rastafarian community have approached the High Court seeking legal protection for their religious use of bhang, arguing that it is a sacred plant central to their spiritual practice...

item.title: Smartcash Launches Campaign to Show Real Value to Nigerian Users
item.description: Smartcash Payment Service Bank, wey be Airtel-owned digital financial platform, don launch new campaign wey dem call 'No Be Cho Cho Cho'. The campaign dey try show say their promises no be empty talk....

item.title: NAIA ranks 7th globally for layover food options
item.description: The Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA) in Manila has been recognized as one of the top airports in the world for layover food, ranking seventh according to the United Kingdom-based Airport Park...

item.title: Maple Leafs snap eight-game losing streak with 6-4 win over Ducks despite Matthews’ injury
item.description: The Toronto Maple Leafs ended an eight-game losing streak with a 6-4 victory over the Anaheim Ducks on Thursday night. William Nylander scored a power-play goal just 36 seconds into the third period t...

item.title: COGTA Committee Raises Concerns on Draft Traditional and Khoisan Leaders Bill
item.description: The Portfolio Committee on Cooperative Governance and Traditional Affairs (COGTA) in South Africa has begun preliminary discussions on the Draft Traditional and Khoisan Leaders Bill, which aims to for...

item.title: Mother mourns after her 16-year-old son fatally stabbed at school in Khutsong
item.description: Gladys Maseko is grieving the tragic loss of her 16-year-old son, Ofentse Ntini, a Grade 9 pupil at Badirile Secondary School in Khutsong, Carletonville, who was fatally stabbed by a fellow learner. O...

item.title: BoT plans new credit guarantee company to support private sector growth
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania (BoT) has started talking with important financial institutions about its plan to create a new credit guarantee company called Tanzania Credit Guarantee PLC. The goal is to make i...

item.title: Pakistan intercepts and neutralises drones from Fitna al-Khawarij with minimal damage
item.description: On 13th March 2026, Pakistan’s Information Ministry confirmed that security forces successfully intercepted and shot down two drones operated by the terrorist group Fitna al-Khawarij. The drones were ...

item.title: Four Chinese fishing vessels intercepted in South African waters for EEZ violations
item.description: On 13 March 2026, South African authorities intercepted four Chinese-flagged fishing vessels—Zhong Yang 231, 232, 233, and 239—within the country’s Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) and territorial waters...

item.title: Pakistan Condemns Closure of Jama Masjid in Srinagar on Last Friday of Ramazan
item.description: The Foreign Office (FO) of Pakistan has strongly condemned the ongoing closure of the historic Jama Masjid in Srinagar, located in Indian-occupied Kashmir, on the last Friday of the holy month of Rama...

item.title: President Tinubu Forms Task Force to Advance Petroleum Sector Reforms and Unlock $5-10bn Liquidity
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has set up a special Presidential Petroleum Reform and Value Optimisation Task Force to push forward the next stage of changes in Nigeria's oil and gas sector. The task for...

item.title: KNCHR Develops Compensation Framework for Protest Victims Within 60 Days
item.description: The Kenya National Commission on Human Rights (KNCHR) has kicked off work on a framework to compensate victims of protests and demonstrations, following a directive from President William Ruto. On Mar...

item.title: Montreal man faces first-degree murder charge in fatal store shooting
item.description: A man in Montreal has been formally charged with first-degree murder following the fatal shooting of a local store owner. Police say the incident occurred under circumstances that are currently under ...
